opp.name: Boral S2C_2026
opp.next_step: Continue engaging key stakeholders and decision-makers while scheduling a solution demo and proposal review to validate requirements, align on scope. Additionally, Luke met Neetu Tickoo, Category Manager – Logistics, at ProcureCon 2026, and she expressed interest in a Zycus demo. Recently connected with Peter Rodwell and he mentioned that things are moving a little slow at their end and hence have asked to be connected during the winter period (tentatively November). Further updates to be shared later once received from Peter.
opp.ais_why: Boral has been actively investing in digital transformation to improve operational efficiency, supply chain resilience, and data-driven decision-making across its construction materials business. Procurement in manufacturing and construction is well-positioned to benefit from AI through supplier collaboration, strategic sourcing, contract lifecycle management, spend analytics, procurement intake, and risk management. Given the scale and complexity of Boral's supplier network and indirect/direct procurement operations, AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions can deliver significant value by automating routine tasks, improving sourcing decisions, and enhancing compliance and visibility. This makes Boral a strong candidate for AI-enabled procurement transformation.

items.quote: No CFO, CPO, VP Procurement, or C-level sponsor identified; all contacts mid-level (Peter Rodwell Procurement Systems Lead, Raj Kiran Program Manager, Neetu Tickoo Category Manager)
No contact has demonstrated advocacy, exec access, or internal selling. Peter Rodwell requested delay; Neetu Tickoo expressed demo interest but no follow-up.
No ROI target, cost savings figure, contract coverage %, or cycle-time reduction stated. Pain is qualitative only.
No visibility into how Boral approves purchases, who must sign off, or what gates must be cleared from scoping to contract.
No discussion of MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec approval, or procurement workflow. No contract expiration or forcing function.
No competitor identified from any source. Cannot position Zycus differentiation or counter rival narratives.

sources: Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026, Raj Kiran and Peter Rodwell: 'Manual sourcing and contract activities are currently executed via email and spreadsheets, prompting the need for an automated source-to-contract solution'

Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026, Adam Hasan question: 'Is there a percentage sort of an idea of how much more you wanna have or how much of the spend is under contract right now, and how much would you like to see the improvement?' (no quantified answer captured)
metrics.narrative: Pain is confirmed and operational but not quantified. Boral currently executes manual sourcing and contract activities via email and spreadsheets, prompting the need for an automated source-to-contract solution (quoted by Raj Kiran and Peter Rodwell on the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call). The business driver is operational inefficiency and lack of process control, but no ROI target, cost savings figure, cycle-time reduction, or contract coverage improvement percentage was stated. Peter Rodwell was asked 'Is there a percentage sort of an idea of how much more you wanna have or how much of the spend is under contract right now, and how much would you like to see the improvement?' but the transcript does not capture a quantified response. The gap: no quantified value case, no baseline metrics (current contract coverage %, manual process cycle time, email/spreadsheet error rate), and no stated business outcome tied to fiscal or operational KPIs. Quantifying the metrics is the immediate next move to build a business case for executive sign-off.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead), Raj Kiran (Program Manager), Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics, Influencer) — no champion role or advocacy evidence
Avoma 23 Mar 2026: Peter and Raj participated in scoping but did not demonstrate advocacy or exec access
Next_Step__c: Neetu Tickoo expressed demo interest at ProcureCon 2026, but no follow-up or advocacy evidence
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead) is the primary contact and led the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call alongside Raj Kiran (Program Manager), but neither has demonstrated advocacy, internal selling, or access to the Economic Buyer. Peter's request to delay until November ('things are moving a little slow at their end') suggests he is reactive rather than driving urgency. Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics) expressed interest in a Zycus demo at ProcureCon 2026 (per Next_Step__c), but there has been no follow-up call or evidence of her actively advancing the deal internally. None of the three contacts have introduced Zycus to senior leadership, shared internal docs, or pushed back on delays to accelerate the timeline. The gap: no coach or advocate who can navigate Boral's internal politics, map the decision-makers, or drive executive sponsorship. Developing a champion (likely Peter or Neetu, depending on who has the strongest internal influence and access) is a top-priority move.

sources: Avoma 23 Mar 2026: no competitor mentioned in scoping call
competition.narrative: No competitor identified from any source. The scoping call on 23 Mar 2026 did not mention any other vendors being evaluated, and Salesforce competition fields (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c) are null. Next_Step__c and Next_Step_History__c contain no references to competitive shortlisting, RFP rounds, or vendor comparisons. This creates a blind spot: Zycus cannot position differentiation (Merlin AI, I2O negotiation, S2C depth vs Coupa/Ariba/Ivalua/GEP) or counter rival narratives. The buyer-requested 5-month delay to November creates a window for a competitor to build relationships and lock in preference while Zycus is on pause. The gap: no visibility into whether Boral is running a sole-source evaluation or a competitive process, and if competitive, who the rivals are and how Boral is scoring vendors. Proactively asking Peter Rodwell about the evaluation approach and competitive landscape during the November reconnection is essential.

sources: Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026, key takeaway: 'Manual sourcing and contract activities are currently executed via email and spreadsheets, prompting the need for an automated source-to-contract solution'

Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026, Adam Hasan question: 'if you continue doing the same operations as you're doing right now in 12 to 24 months time, what would happen to the business?' (buyer acknowledged unsustainability but did not quantify)
identify_pain.narrative: Boral's operational pain is clearly articulated: manual sourcing and contract activities are currently executed via email and spreadsheets, creating inefficiency, lack of process control, and compliance risk. Raj Kiran and Peter Rodwell confirmed this pain on the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call. When asked 'if you continue doing the same operations as you're doing right now in 12 to 24 months time, what would happen to the business?', the buyer acknowledged the status quo is unsustainable but did not quantify the impact (no stated cost, error rate, compliance incident count, or cycle-time baseline). The pain owner is Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead) and Raj Kiran (Program Manager), both operationally accountable for sourcing and contracting process improvement. The pain is real and acknowledged, but it has not been escalated to an executive sponsor or tied to a fiscal/compliance forcing function, which limits urgency.

sources: Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026: discussion of external approvals in the workflow system, but no contracting/legal process mentioned
No Current_Contract_Expiration__c or compliance forcing function in Salesforce
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec approval, and procurement channel are all unknown. The scoping call on 23 Mar 2026 focused on system scope and integration but did not touch MSA/SOW, legal redlines, security review, or procurement workflow. No contract expiration date, renewal forcing function, or compliance deadline was mentioned that would drive urgency. The buyer was asked about external stakeholder approvals ('if there are external stakeholders across the business who will need to log in to approve or do a contract signature or something like that'), and the response indicated minimal external approval needs beyond the 25-user core team, but no formal contracting process was discussed. The gap: no visibility into Boral's legal/procurement timeline, whether an MSA is required, whether InfoSec/compliance must sign off, or what the paper trail from proposal to signature looks like. Understanding the paper process is essential before any commercial or contract discussion.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead), Raj Kiran (Program Manager), Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics, Influencer) — no EB/DM/Exec Sponsor role
Avoma 23 Mar 2026: scoping call with Raj and Peter, no exec participation, no budget discussion
Next_Step__c: Peter Rodwell delay request, no mention of approver or exec sponsor
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified after 98 days in Qualified. All three named contacts are mid-level: Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead), Raj Kiran (Program Manager), and Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics, marked Influencer in Salesforce). No CFO, CPO, VP Procurement, or C-level sponsor has appeared in any call, Salesforce contact role, or next-step log. The scoping call on 23 Mar 2026 was purely operational (system scope, user count, integration points) with no budget authority discussion, no commercial topic, and no mention of who controls the $260k investment decision. Peter Rodwell mentioned 'things are moving a little slow at their end' when requesting the November delay, but did not name an approver or escalation path. The gap: no visibility into who approves the budget, whether this is a single-signature or multi-approval decision, or whether the mid-level contacts have spending authority. Mapping the Economic Buyer is the critical next move to validate budget and timeline.

sources: Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026, Adam Hasan question: 'Are you planning on having a source to contract solution up and running by 2027, next financial year?' (no definitive answer captured)
Next_Step__c: Peter Rodwell requested reconnection in November, citing slow progress, but did not outline internal gates or approvers
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. The scoping call on 23 Mar 2026 covered technical scope and user count but did not discuss approval workflow, procurement steps, or timeline milestones. When asked 'Are you planning on having a source to contract solution up and running by 2027, next financial year?', the buyer did not commit to a timeline. Peter Rodwell's delay request to November 2026 ('things are moving a little slow at their end') signals internal uncertainty or competing priorities, but he did not explain the internal process, who must approve, or what gates must be cleared before moving to vendor selection or contracting. No mention of legal review, InfoSec review, procurement approval tiers, or whether this is a sole-source or competitive evaluation. The gap: no visibility into how Boral makes purchasing decisions, whether multiple approvals are required, or what the milestone sequence is from scoping to contract signature. Clarifying the decision process and approval chain is a top-priority move.

sources: Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026: 'A Phase 1 implementation will cover sourcing, contracting, and supplier onboarding for the central team of 25 power users with minimal integration touchpoints'
Avoma transcript 23 Mar 2026: 'Oracle EBS is confirmed as the sole ERP integration point in Phase 1, with no immediate plans to upgrade to Fusion or integrate Maximo'
decision_criteria.narrative: Phase 1 scope and technical fit criteria are confirmed from the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call: automated sourcing, contracting, and supplier onboarding for 25 central power users; Oracle EBS as the sole ERP integration point (no Fusion upgrade, no Maximo integration in Phase 1); modules required are iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, and ML iAnalyze. The buyer confirmed no immediate plans to integrate other systems and minimal external stakeholder approval workflow needs beyond the core team. However, no formal evaluation criteria, vendor scorecard, or RFP/RFI requirements were discussed. No mention of AI/Merlin capabilities, no discussion of competitive bake-off criteria, no stated technical proof points (uptime SLA, data security, API performance), and no business case criteria (ROI threshold, payback period, user adoption targets). The gap: Boral has not articulated how they will choose between vendors, what the deal-breaker requirements are, or what the formal decision process entails. Validating decision criteria through a deeper discovery or BRD review is the next step.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Differentiated where it counts — No AI appetite signal captured. AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null. Scoping call on 23 Mar 2026 confirmed ML iAnalyze in Phase 1 scope but did not discuss AI use cases, Merlin, or I2O. Buyer has not articulated AI needs, RFP AI rating, or AI-driven ROI expectations. This is a hygiene gap (AI fit discussion not yet held) rather than a knowledge gap (buyer is AI-resistant). Tier cannot be assessed without deeper discovery.
bullets.text: Differentiated where it counts — No AI appetite signal captured.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning untested. Zycus ML iAnalyze is in Phase 1 product scope (per Products__c and scoping call 23 Mar 2026), but no discussion of Merlin AI negotiation agent, I2O sourcing intelligence, or AI-driven process automation during the scoping call. No AIS score, no buyer AI appetite signal. The buyer's pain (manual email/spreadsheet processes) is a natural fit for AI-driven automation, but the value narrative has not been articulated. Positioning Merlin and I2O as differentiation vs competitors (if any emerge) and as ROI driver for exec sponsor is a missed opportunity that must be corrected in the November demo.

The gap: no visibility into who approves the budget, whether this is a single-signature or multi-approval decision, or whether the mid-level contacts have spending authority. Mapping the Economic Buyer is the critical next move to validate budget and timeline.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 21 is carried by differentiation strength +1.00 (weight 20); momentum 62 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: preference strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 62 (moving forward): Next step fresh + dated milestones. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 42 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (21), nudged by commitment 42 and momentum 62, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

items.commitment: Reconnect with Peter Rodwell and team during winter period (tentatively November) to resume evaluation
items.deliverable: Reconnect with Peter Rodwell and team during winter period (tentatively November) to resume evaluation

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning untested. Zycus ML iAnalyze is in Phase 1 product scope (per Products__c and scoping call 23 Mar 2026), but no discussion of Merlin AI negotiation agent, I2O sourcing intelligence, or AI-driven process automation during the scoping call. No AIS score, no buyer AI appetite signal. The buyer's pain (manual email/spreadsheet processes) is a natural fit for AI-driven automation, but the value narrative has not been articulated. Positioning Merlin and I2O as differentiation vs competitors (if any emerge) and as ROI driver for exec sponsor is a missed opportunity that must be corrected in the November demo.

items.quote: Manual sourcing and contract activities are currently executed via email and spreadsheets, prompting the need for an automated source-to-contract solution
items.criterion: Automated source-to-contract solution to replace manual email and spreadsheet processes

items.quote: A Phase 1 implementation will cover sourcing, contracting, and supplier onboarding for the central team of 25 power users with minimal integration touchpoints
items.criterion: Phase 1 scope: sourcing, contracting, supplier onboarding for 25 central power users

items.quote: Oracle EBS is confirmed as the sole ERP integration point in Phase 1, with no immediate plans to upgrade to Fusion or integrate Maximo
items.criterion: Oracle EBS integration (sole ERP in Phase 1, no Fusion or Maximo)

items.quote: Is there a percentage sort of an idea of how much more you wanna have or how much of the spend is under contract right now, and how much would you like to see the improvement? (no quantified answer captured)
items.criterion: Quantified business case (ROI, cost savings, contract coverage improvement %) to justify $260k investment to exec sponsor

items.quote: Peter Rodwell requested delay to November 2026 ('things are moving a little slow at their end'), no forcing function mentioned
items.criterion: Urgency driver or forcing function (contract expiration, fiscal deadline, compliance mandate) to drive timeline

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Phase 1 technical fit is confirmed: Zycus iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, and ML iAnalyze align with Boral's stated need to automate manual email/spreadsheet sourcing and contracting for 25 central power users with Oracle EBS integration. However, broader business-case fit (ROI justification, executive value narrative, urgency driver) is weak: no quantified metrics, no exec sponsor, no forcing function, and a buyer-requested 5-month delay signals low internal priority.

hard.ais_why: Boral has been actively investing in digital transformation to improve operational efficiency, supply chain resilience, and data-driven decision-making across its construction materials business. Procurement in manufacturing and construction is well-positioned to benefit from AI through supplier collaboration, strategic sourcing, contract lifecycle management, spend analytics, procurement intake, and risk management. Given the scale and complexity of Boral's supplier network and indirect/direct procurement operations, AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions can deliver significant value by automating routine tasks, improving sourcing decisions, and enhancing compliance and visibility. This makes Boral a strong candidate for AI-enabled procurement transformation.
hard.next_step:
Continue engaging key stakeholders and decision-makers while scheduling a solution demo and proposal review to validate requirements, align on scope. Additionally, Luke met Neetu Tickoo, Category Manager – Logistics, at ProcureCon 2026, and she expressed interest in a Zycus demo.

Recently connected with Peter Rodwell and he mentioned that things are moving a little slow at their end and hence have asked to be connected during the winter period (tentatively November). Further updates to be shared later once received from Peter.

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 7 day(s) ago (2026-06-24). Buyer EMAIL RECEIVED 22 day(s) ago (2026-06-09) — a real two-way reply from the buyer (NOT rep outreach); treat as genuine engagement.

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: Map Economic Buyer and decision-maker authority before November reconnection. After 98 days in Qualified, all contacts are mid-level (Procurement Systems Lead, Program Manager, Category Manager) with no CFO/CPO/VP visibility. Peter Rodwell's delay request signals lack of exec sponsorship and urgency. Without exec access, deal lacks budget authority and forcing function. Luke (or partner) must identify who controls the $260k decision and secure intro before demo/proposal in November.

deltas.subject: Develop champion with exec access and internal advocacy. Peter Rodwell and Raj Kiran are engaged but reactive (Peter requested 5-month delay). deltas.subject: Quantify business case to build exec-level urgency and justify $260k investment. Pain is confirmed (manual email/spreadsheet sourcing and contracting) but not quantified (no ROI, cost savings, contract coverage %, cycle-time baseline). Without metrics, exec sponsor will deprioritize. Before November demo, work with Peter/Raj to baseline current process cost and project ROI (savings, compliance improvement, cycle-time reduction) to anchor exec conversation and create forcing function.

Luke must assess who has strongest internal influence and groom them to drive urgency, map stakeholders, and counter any stealth competitor during the pause.

value.flag: Quantify business case to build exec-level urgency and justify $260k investment. Pain is confirmed (manual email/spreadsheet sourcing and contracting) but not quantified (no ROI, cost savings, contract coverage %, cycle-time baseline). Without metrics, exec sponsor will deprioritize. Before November demo, work with Peter/Raj to baseline current process cost and project ROI (savings, compliance improvement, cycle-time reduction) to anchor exec conversation and create forcing function.

business_case.evidence: Pain confirmed: manual email/spreadsheet sourcing and contracting (Raj Kiran and Peter Rodwell, 23 Mar 2026). No quantified ROI, cost savings, contract coverage %, or cycle-time reduction stated.

gaps: Only 1 buyer call (scoping call 23 Mar 2026, 100 days ago); no discovery depth, no exec participation, no follow-up calls to validate MEDDPICC, quantify business case, or map decision process
Partner-led deal: Luke Dougherty (owner) works through partner channel; limited direct Zycus-buyer engagement visible in Salesforce/Avoma
No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or executive sponsor identified across 98 days in Qualified
No quantified business case (ROI, cost savings, contract coverage %, cycle-time baseline) from scoping call or any follow-up
No visibility into decision process, approval chain, paper process (legal, InfoSec, procurement), or fiscal forcing function
No competitor identified from any source (cannot position differentiation or assess competitive threat)
AI positioning untested: Merlin and I2O capabilities not discussed on scoping call despite ML iAnalyze in product scope
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Merlin AI')

validation gate: hard.next_step 'Continue engaging key stakeholders and decision-makers while scheduling a solution demo and proposal review to validate requirements, align on scope. Additionally, Luke met Neetu Tickoo, Category Manager – Logistics, at ProcureCon 2026, and she expressed interest in a Zycus demo. Recently connected with Peter Rodwell and he mentioned that things are moving a little slow at their end and hence have asked to be connected during the winter period (tentatively November). Further updates to be shared later once received from Peter.' diverges from Salesforce '

customer_preference.evidence: Single scoping call 23 Mar 2026 was purely technical (scope, user count, integration); no preference signal, no Zycus-specific praise, no competitive comparison. Neetu Tickoo expressed demo interest at ProcureCon 2026 but no follow-up.
